findings.evidence: `rg -n "isEffective_coe|weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup|eq_zero_of_weighted"` shows only the unweighted subgroup eliminator at line 352 and weighted kernel facts at lines 455/464, plus the raw weighted-degree lemma at line 318, but no subgroup-level weighted eliminator.
findings.fix: Add a public lemma near `weightedDegree_coe_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup`, e.g. `isEffective_coe_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup_eq_zero {w} (hw : ∀ x, 0 < w x) {D : weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup w} (hD : IsEffective (D : WeilDivisor X)) : (D : WeilDivisor X) = 0`, proved from `hD.eq_zero_of_weightedDegree_eq_zero_of_pos hw (weightedDegree_coe_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup w D)`.
findings.issue: `weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup` lacks the weighted analogue of `isEffective_coe_degreeZeroSubgroup_eq_zero`, so users of positive residue-field weights cannot eliminate an effective element of the weighted degree-zero subgroup without manually unpacking the kernel.

summary: The new unweighted public API has the expected effective degree-zero eliminator, but the parallel weighted-degree-zero subgroup API is missing despite the file already developing weighted kernels for the stated residue-degree target.
